title: smyrnium cordifolium
intro: Smyrnium cordifolium, also known as wild celery, is a perennial plant species belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae). The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic effects. The health benefits of Smyrnium cordifolium include treating urinary tract infections, kidney stones, and digestive issues, as well as reducing fever and pain. Smyrnium cordifolium is often prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, and is also used in traditional medicine to create salves and ointments for topical application.
